observed_failure.prior_local_claim: self-host validates the repository-root current project-adoption manifest and managed AGENTS block
observed_failure.observed_result: the compiled artifact path pointed inside the skill directory, where no manifest exists, and the declared audit owner ran only unit tests instead of auditing the current repository state

classification.owner: SkillGuard self-host contract and project-adoption route
classification.error_signature: Self-host reaches project adoption with a non-canonical manifest path or without replaying project-audit.

root_cause_backpropagation.supported_cause: the self-contract test asserted generated contract and manifest paths but omitted the project-adoption artifact path and exact audit command
root_cause_backpropagation.would_have_failed_if: the regression had compared the project-adoption artifact path with the repository-root protocol and required the self-host audit owner to invoke project-audit on the current root
root_cause_backpropagation.new_model_item: a portable project-adoption claim is licensed only by the canonical repository-root manifest plus a current project-audit replay
root_cause_backpropagation.owner_code_contract: contract-source project-adoption artifact -> self-host artifact validation -> project-audit current-state replay

generalized_repair.rule: Bind governed project artifacts to their declared ownership root and require the terminal verifier to execute the real current-state audit rather than a test-only proxy.
generalized_repair.observed_regression: the self-host contract now resolves the repository-root .skillguard/project.json artifact and the current project-audit owner passes against the live manifest and AGENTS block
generalized_repair.same_class_regression: the focused self-contract regression pins both the canonical artifact path and the exact project-audit command
generalized_repair.negative_boundary: do not add an alternate manifest path, compatibility reader, fallback audit, or installation retry

required_revalidation: project-adoption unit tests and current project-audit

revalidation_evidence.focused_regressions: 8 tests and 2 subtests passed across the self-contract assertion and project-adoption suite
revalidation_evidence.project_audit: current repository-root manifest, managed AGENTS block, skillguard, and skillguard-global-router all passed
revalidation_evidence.model_and_contract: FlowGuard self-contract model and TestMesh passed; deterministic contract check returned contract 1B6229A5B87896ED003E8E90F94F601FF08FA45AA8AF8325E1B3E3309B321689 and manifest B66D37941D884041B63CDAE5918FE4E37CCF87A6B1431DA895120E4B933CFEEC
revalidation_evidence.installation: staged verification passed and committed global installation transaction install-9f924484715b4f96bc821c5220d3eab7 with the previous generation retained as rollback backup
revalidation_evidence.self_host: run-c7d27abbdc3a4df2f4d1 passed enforced closure closure-343c65572e024e6b462ce507 with current replay verification

claim_boundary: The repair is validated for the current canonical SkillGuard source, repository project-adoption state, staged and active two-member installation, and replay-verified self-host closure. It does not prove GitHub publication, unrelated target skills, or future AI behavior.
